message:
fix(session-restore): shadow-proof checkpoint selection and partial restore resilience

Session restore could select empty "startup" checkpoints (which only
record pane ID mappings) over older capture checkpoints that contain
actual pane state data. Partial restores also incorrectly marked the
source session as clean, preventing retry.

Checkpoint selection:
- load_latest_checkpoint() now uses an ORDER BY that prioritizes
  checkpoints with pane_state rows over empty ones, falling back to
  checkpoint_at DESC within each tier
- detect() iterates all unclean session candidates, loading and
  validating each checkpoint instead of blindly picking the most recent
  session — sessions with no checkpoints, empty checkpoints, or
  unreadable data are skipped with warnings
- Best candidate is the one with the most recent USABLE checkpoint, not
  just the most recent session timestamp

Partial restore handling:
- restore() only marks the source session as clean (shutdown_clean=true)
  when ALL panes restore successfully
- If any panes fail, the source session stays unclean so detect() can
  find it again for retry
- format_restore_summary() now shows "partially restored" status with
  failed pane count when applicable

Tests:
- SplitFailOnceWezterm mock: WeztermInterface impl that fails the first
  split_pane call, succeeds subsequent ones
- Test: capture checkpoint preferred over newer startup checkpoint
- Test: fallback to newest empty checkpoint when no usable ones exist
- Test: detect() skips sessions without checkpoints
- Test: detect() prefers most recent usable checkpoint across sessions
- Test: partial restore leaves session unclean for retry, retry detects
  it again
- Test: format_restore_summary shows partial status
- Existing detect() test updated to include checkpoint with pane state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
